prompt:
You operate a healthcare world model.
World: a discharge-readiness world with readmission risk; actions discharge, schedule_followup, readmit
Rules: Every step advances the clock by exactly one day.; While 'admitted', discharge-readiness rises by 10 per day (capped at 100); 'length_of_stay' counts ward days.; 'discharge' is only valid for an admitted patient; it sends them home and sets readmission_risk = (100 - readiness)/100.; A scheduled follow-up multiplies readmission_risk by 0.6, rewarding continuity of care; it can be booked before or after discharge.; 'readmit' is only valid for a discharged patient; it returns them to the ward, lowers readiness by 25, raises risk by 0.2, clears the follow-up, and increments the readmission count.; readmission_risk is always clamped to [0, 1].

Rules: Every step advances the clock by exactly one day.; While 'admitted', discharge-readiness rises by 10 per day (capped at 100); 'length_of_stay' counts ward days.; 'discharge' is only valid for an admitted patient; it sends them home and sets readmission_risk = (100 - readiness)/100.; A scheduled follow-up multiplies readmission_risk by 0.6, rewarding continuity of care; it can be booked before or after discharge.; 'readmit' is only valid for a discharged patient; it returns them to the ward, lowers readiness by 25, raises risk by 0.2, clears the follow-up, and increments the readmission count.; readmission_risk is always clamped to [0, 1].

World: a vaccine cold-chain world: temperature and stock; actions ship, store, discard on temperature excursion
Rules: The safe storage window is 2 C to 8 C; outside it the unit is in a temperature excursion.; store receives a 10-dose batch and runs refrigeration, relaxing the temperature toward the 5 C setpoint.; ship releases up to a 10-dose batch downstream, but only while the temperature is within the safe window.; Without active cooling (ship/discard) the temperature drifts warmer toward 22 C ambient by 1.5 C per step.; discard writes off all on-hand stock; it is the response to stock compromised by a temperature excursion.; Cumulative shipped and discarded dose counts never decrease; stock is never negative.

Rules: 'list' admits the next queued (or perceived) candidate onto the waitlist, kept sorted by urgency (desc), then waiting time, then id.; 'match' allocates one available organ to the most urgent ABO-compatible candidate needing that organ type, recording it as the pending match; if no compatible pair exists the pending match is cleared.; ABO compatibility: O donates to anyone, A to A/AB, B to B/AB, AB to AB only; the organ type must also equal the candidate's needed organ.; 'transplant' executes the pending match: it removes the organ and recipient, appends a completed transplant, and clears the pending match.; Organs and waitlist seats are conserved -- a transplant consumes exactly one of each

You operate a healthcare world model.
World: an ED triage world: patients arrive with an acuity level and wait; actions triage, assign_bed, discharge; sicker patients are seen first
Rules: Acuity is 1..5 where 1 is the most acute (sickest) and 5 the least.; Each patient advances waiting -> triaged -> in_bed -> discharged.; Every action advances the clock by 1 and ages the wait of every patient still waiting or triaged.; 'triage' moves the sickest waiting patient to triaged.; 'assign_bed' moves the sickest triaged patient into a bed, only if a bed is free (beds_occupied < beds_total).; 'discharge' discharges the sickest in-bed patient, freeing a bed and incrementing discharged.; Sicker patients are always selected first; ties break by longest wait, then lowest id.; A perceived arrival (pending_acuity in 1..5) is admitted as a new waiting patient on the next step.

Rules: Acuity is 1..5 where 1 is the most acute (sickest) and 5 the least.; Each patient advances waiting -> triaged -> in_bed -> discharged.; Every action advances the clock by 1 and ages the wait of every patient still waiting or triaged.; 'triage' moves the sickest waiting patient to triaged.; 'assign_bed' moves the sickest triaged patient into a bed, only if a bed is free (beds_occupied < beds_total).; 'discharge' discharges the sickest in-bed patient, freeing a bed and incrementing discharged.; Sicker patients are always selected first; ties break by longest wait, then lowest id.; A perceived arrival (pending_acuity in 1..5) is admitted as a new waiting patient on the next step.

Rules: Acuity is 1..5 where 1 is the most acute (sickest) and 5 the least.; Each patient advances waiting -> triaged -> in_bed -> discharged.; Every action advances the clock by 1 and ages the wait of every patient still waiting or triaged.; 'triage' moves the sickest waiting patient to triaged.; 'assign_bed' moves the sickest triaged patient into a bed, only if a bed is free (beds_occupied < beds_total).; 'discharge' discharges the sickest in-bed patient, freeing a bed and incrementing discharged.; Sicker patients are always selected first; ties break by longest wait, then lowest id.; A perceived arrival (pending_acuity in 1..5) is admitted as a new waiting patient on the next step.

World: a sepsis early-warning world: vitals drift and a risk score rises; actions monitor, escalate, treat to lower risk
Rules: Every tick the vitals drift away from their healthy baselines (heart rate, respiratory rate, and temperature rise; systolic blood pressure falls; lactate rises) -- the patient is deteriorating toward sepsis.; The risk score (0-100) is a deterministic function of how far the vitals have strayed from baseline; only low blood pressure and high lactate count as harmful.; 'monitor' raises the monitoring level (max 2); closer observation slows further drift but does not itself reverse the vitals.; 'escalate' summons the rapid-response team: monitoring jumps to continuous, drift slows further, and any subsequent 'treat' is markedly more effective.; 'treat' administers antibiotics and fluids, pullin

Rules: A claim enters the world via `submit`, starting in the `submitted` state.; `review` moves the oldest `submitted` claim to `under_review`.; `approve` moves the oldest `under_review` claim to `approved` and pays its amount.; `deny` moves the oldest `under_review` claim to `denied`; nothing is paid.; A claim must be reviewed before it can be approved or denied; a `submitted` claim cannot be approved or denied directly.; `approved` and `denied` are terminal states; a claim never leaves them.; An action whose targeted eligible claim does not exist is a no-op.; Without an explicit `claim_id`, each action acts on the oldest eligible claim, so the queue is adjudicated first-in, first-out.

You operate a healthcare world model.
World: an operating-room scheduling world: cases with durations and rooms; actions book, delay, cancel without double-booking
Rules: A case occupies the half-open interval [start, start + duration) in exactly one room.; book schedules a pending case; it is allowed only if the interval lies within the room's open hours [0, horizon) and does not overlap any case already booked in that room. Such a request never double-books: an infeasible booking is refused and increments 'rejected' instead, leaving the schedule unchanged.; When book omits room/start, the earliest non-overlapping slot (scanning rooms in order, earliest start first) is chosen deterministically.; delay moves an already-booked case to a strictly later start in the same room, only when the shifted interval still fits without overlap; otherwise it is refused.; c

Rules: A case occupies the half-open interval [start, start + duration) in exactly one room.; book schedules a pending case; it is allowed only if the interval lies within the room's open hours [0, horizon) and does not overlap any case already booked in that room. Such a request never double-books: an infeasible booking is refused and increments 'rejected' instead, leaving the schedule unchanged.; When book omits room/start, the earliest non-overlapping slot (scanning rooms in order, earliest start first) is chosen deterministically.; delay moves an already-booked case to a strictly later start in the same room, only when the shifted interval still fits without overlap; otherwise it is refused.; c

World: a hospital infection-spread world (SIR-like) with isolation capacity; actions isolate, treat, screen
